title: Tutorial Entry
description: Metadata for a Builder Weekly tutorial entry. One JSON file per entry, living at entries/{id}/metadata.json alongside the entry's tutorial.md.

id.description: Unique identifier, lowercase kebab-case. Example: accountability-loop.

title.description: Human-readable title. Declarative statement, not a question. No marketing words.

slug.description: URL-safe slug. Usually identical to id but kept as a separate field so the two can diverge if needed.

pillar.description: Parent pillar this entry belongs to. Operational is the catch-all for entries that don't fit a single pillar.

clusterTags.description: Cluster tags within the pillar. Lowercase kebab-case. Used for cross-entry navigation on the site.

soulLine.description: One sentence naming explicitly why an agent beats a human at this task. No hedges, no generalities. Must reference a specific measurable advantage (speed, recall, consistency, cost, availability).

beforeState.description: What the task looks like without the agent. Concrete enough that a reader recognizes their own workflow in it.

afterState.description: What the task looks like with the agent. Concrete enough that a reader can evaluate whether the trade-off is worth it for them.

status.description: Current lifecycle status. queued: in topics.json, no entry yet. drafted: entry exists, hasn't passed verifier. verified: passed all automated checks, pending human review. published: live on the site. stale: freshness window elapsed, needs re-verification.

author.description: GitHub username of the original author. For pipeline-generated entries, the engine's dedicated bot account.

contributors.description: GitHub usernames of contributors whose PRs against this entry landed after the original.

tools.description: Tools used in the tutorial. Every tool must be in Builder Weekly's Agent Index (github.com/thebuilderweekly/the-agent-index) or in the well-known allowlist enforced by the merge gate.

createdAt.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) when the entry was first created.

lastVerifiedAt.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) when the entry last passed the verifier. Advances on every re-verification run.

freshnessWindowDays.description: Days from lastVerifiedAt before the entry is flagged as stale and scheduled for re-verification.
